title: [CI:DOCS] Add more information and examples on podman and pipes
body:
Improve the documentation to help users to know proper way to
use podman within a pipe.

Helps Prevent: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8916

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

We should also mention it prevents redirection of standard output (combines
STDOUT and STDERR, can insert control characters, can hang pipes).

On Wed, Jan 13, 2021 at 08:46 Ed Santiago <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> *@edsantiago* commented on this pull request.
>
> LGTM but I'm picky; feel free to ignore my nits.
> ------------------------------
>
> In docs/source/markdown/podman-run.1.md
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8962#discussion_r556528876>:
>
> > @@ -1011,8 +1011,14 @@ When set to **true**, Podman will allocate a pseudo-tty and attach to the standa
>  input of the container. This can be used, for example, to run a throwaway
>  interactive shell. The default is **false**.
>
> -**NOTE**: The **-t** option is incompatible with a redirection of the Podman client
> -standard input.
> +**NOTE**: The **-t** option is incompatible with a redirection of the Podman client standard input.
> +
> +```
> +Use
> +echo "asdf" | podman run --rm -i someimage /bin/cat
> +Versus
> +echo "asdf" | podman run --rm -it someimage /bin/cat
>
> Both sample commands have equal visual prominence, making it hard for my
> eye to figure out which is the correct one. I'm a fan of "show what to do,
> and hide the taboos". Could I suggest something like:
>
> ...incompatible with a redirection of the Podman client standard input.
> That is, when feeding input to podman, use -i only, not -it:
>
>     $ echo asdf | podman run --rm -i someimage /bin/cat
>
> ------------------------------
>
> In docs/source/markdown/podman-run.1.md
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8962#discussion_r556528971>:
>
> > @@ -1528,6 +1534,13 @@ weight by **--blkio-weight-device** flag. Use the following command:
>  $ podman run -it --blkio-weight-device "/dev/sda:200" ubuntu
>  ```
>
> +### Using a podman container from within a piped command
>
> I'm not entirely sure this title reflects the intention. I find it hard to
> parse. Maybe "Using a podman container *with input from a pipe*"? Or
> "Piping command output to a podman container"?
>
> —
